name: Thorne
description: Elderly storyteller of The Moist Oyster -- frail-seeming but with penetrating blue eyes and a cane that hides a blade. Secretly a former Order of the Lake initiate and Dragon-Knight-raid survivor with peerless historical lore (Dragon Kings era 92) and knowledge of possession-ritual weaknesses.
narrative: Most evenings Thorne holds court at The Moist Oyster, collecting tales and gossip from new arrivals. He looks frail, but his blue eyes are too vibrant for his age and his cane conceals a blade. He is a survivor: a former member of the Order of the Lake who lived through the early Dragon Knight raids. His value to the players is knowledge -- he recognizes Baron Hanzo's rituals as attempts to recreate ancient draconic transformations, he knows where possession bindings are vulnerable, and he can supply the historical context of the Dragon Kings era that the whole campaign turns on. Behind the storyteller's warmth sits a sharp, wary mind and an extensive web of contacts.

equipment.name: Cane (disguised weapon)
equipment.notes: Stun Location; concealed blade option 1d6 Bleed/Impale

extras.class: Storyteller / former Order of the Lake (Cleric-trained)
extras.role: Elderly storyteller at The Moist Oyster; secretly a former member of the Order of the Lake and survivor of the early Dragon Knight raids.
extras.tells: Appears frail, but has penetrating blue eyes too vibrant for his age; his cane doubles as a disguised weapon; maintains an extensive contact network.
extras.value: Recognizes the Baron's rituals as attempts to recreate ancient draconic transformations; knows vulnerabilities in possession rituals; provides crucial historical context (Dragon Kings era).
extras.magic_tradition: Lapsed Order of the Lake initiate -- retains deep ritual/water lore and Willpower; light blessings at most, no active battle magic.
